title: Refactor: replace StringInSlice with slices.Contains
body:
This PR refactors code to use [slices.Contains](https://pkg.go.dev/golang.org/x/exp/slices#Contains) instead of `StringInSlice`, [slices.Index](https://pkg.go.dev/golang.org/x/exp/slices#Index) instead of `IndexOfStringInSlice`.

Other changes:
- remove `util.StringInSlice`and `util.IndexOfStringInSlice` from `pkg/util` package;
- remove `utils.StringInSlice` from `test/utils` package;
- remove `test.StringInSlice` from `pkg/bindings/test` package.

When we upgrade to Go 1.21, `golang.org/x/exp/slices` will be replaced by [`slices`](https://pkg.go.dev/slices) from std.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
Almost there:
```
pkg/util/utils_test.go:17:2: var `sliceData` is unused (unused)
	sliceData = []string{"one", "two", "three", "four"}
```

Thanks for the great contributions!
